analysis.summary: The US-Israel war with Iran, which began on February 28, 2026 with joint strikes announced by President Trump, has entered its fourth week amid simultaneous military escalation and ceasefire diplomacy. The Trump administration offered Iran a 15-point ceasefire plan, reportedly transmitted via Pakistan, while deploying additional troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East. Iran's government and military officials have consistently denied that any negotiations are taking place, with the IRGC calling US claims of talks 'fake news,' even as markets reacted positively to reports of a possible deal.
